# AppCubic

> An applied AI venture studio. AppCubic builds agentic systems, turns research into working products, and advises technical teams.

AppCubic is an applied AI venture studio, building since 2019. The work sits where prototypes have to become dependable: agent workflows, scientific and analytical tooling, and the products that grow out of them. Much of the studio's agent and research tooling is open source under App Automaton.

## What the studio does

- Agentic systems: reliable agent workflows with clear tool boundaries and honest evaluation, carried toward production.
- Research to product: scientific and analytical workflows turned into systems people can run, built for correctness and repeatability.
- Venture studio and advisory: early product strategy, technical diligence, grant support, and incubation for AI-first products.

## App Automaton, the open-source workshop

App Automaton is the studio's open-source GitHub organization. Overview at https://appcubic.com/appautomaton/ and https://appautomaton.github.io.

- [latex-arxiv-SKILL](https://appautomaton.github.io/latex-arxiv-SKILL/): a portable Agent Skill that turns a topic into an arXiv-ready ML review paper, gated and verified citation by citation.
- [mlx-atomistic](https://appautomaton.github.io/mlx-atomistic/): an Apple-Silicon molecular dynamics and density-functional-theory runtime on MLX and Metal, with no CUDA, server, or cloud.
- [docker-for-apple-container](https://appautomaton.github.io/docker-for-apple-container/): a stateless docker shim over Apple's native container CLI on macOS, with no Docker Desktop.
- [agent-designer](https://appautomaton.github.io/agent-designer/): the skills workspace, with issue-driven workflows and bridge scripts for agent-to-agent delegation.
- More across the organization: https://github.com/appautomaton

## Links

- [Research](https://research.appcubic.com): research systems.
- [GitHub](https://github.com/appautomaton): open-source work.
- [Benji Peng](https://benji.renocrypt.com): founder.
